semantic.description: An integral of a rational function is an indefinite integral of the form \( \int \frac{N(x)}{D(x)}\,dx \), where \( N(x) \) and \( D(x) \) are polynomials with real coefficients and \( D(x) \) is not identically zero.

semantic.statement: For polynomials \( N(x) \) and nonzero \( D(x) \), polynomial division yields \( N(x)=Q(x)D(x)+R(x) \) with \( \deg R(x)<\deg D(x) \). Consequently, \(\int \frac{N(x)}{D(x)}\,dx=\int Q(x)\,dx+\int \frac{R(x)}{D(x)}\,dx. \)
For real constants \( a,b,c \) with \( a \neq 0 \), \(\int \frac{c}{ax+b}\,dx = \frac{c}{a}\ln|ax+b| + k \) on every interval where \( ax+b \neq 0 \).

semantic.description: A repeated linear factor is a factor of the form \( (x-r)^k \) with multiplicity \( k \geq 2 \) appearing in the denominator of a rational function.

semantic.term: Partial Fraction Form for Repeated Linear Factors
semantic.statement: If the denominator contains the factor \( (x-r)^k \), then the corresponding contribution to the partial fraction decomposition is \( \frac{A_1}{x-r}+\frac{A_2}{(x-r)^2}+\cdots+\frac{A_k}{(x-r)^k}. \)

semantic.description: A quadratic polynomial \( ax^2+bx+c \) is irreducible over \( \mathbb{R} \) when its discriminant satisfies \( b^2-4ac<0 \), so that it has no real roots.

semantic.term: Partial Fraction Form for Irreducible Quadratic Factors
semantic.statement: If the denominator of a rational function contains an irreducible quadratic factor \( ax^2+bx+c \), then the corresponding contribution to the partial fraction decomposition has the form \( \frac{Ax+B}{ax^2+bx+c}. \)
